title: Letters from Snail's Pace Village
synopsis: When the village clock stops tick-tocking, friendly ghost Boo and wise rabbit Clover exchange letters to solve the mystery. In Snail's Pace Village where even snails are speedy, they discover that sometimes the simplest solutions sparkle brightest!

pages.text:
Dear Clover Cottontail,

The big TICK-TOCK clock has gone silent! No tick! No tock! No ting-a-ling morning chimes!

I was floating by the tower when it happened - CLUNK! Everything stopped. The clock hands froze at nine-fifteen, and now nobody knows when to wake up, when to eat breakfast, or when to race snails!

Even Speedy Sam Snail zoomed past me three times asking, 'Is it morning? Is it noon? When do I nap?'

The villagers are walking in slow-motion circles, slower than usual. Mrs. Maple tried to bake bread but forgot when to take it out. Mr. Turtle started his daily walk but doesn't know if he should turn around yet!

Can you help? Your bunny ears hear things I miss!

Tick-less and tock-less,
Boo

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes floating near a tall silent clock tower. Confused villagers walk in slow circles below while snails with question marks above their shells zoom around. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Boo floating by a silent clock tower with confused villagers below

pages.text:
Dear Sweet Boo,

Oh my carrots and clover! A clock mystery!

I hopped-hop-hopped to the tower right away. My sensitive bunny ears heard something you missed - a tiny 'buzz-buzz-buzz' sound coming from inside the clock!

I wiggled my whiskers and sniffed around. There's a small door at the bottom with beautiful brass hinges. But it's locked tight-tight-tight with a key-shaped hole!

The buzz-buzz-buzzing gets louder when I put my ear close. Something's definitely moving in there - but what? And where's the key?

I asked Granny Toad (she's lived here longest), and she croaked, 'The clockmaker left the key somewhere safe when he moved away. Look for something that jingles!'

But Boo, nothing jingles in Snail's Pace Village except...

Wait! I have an idea! Meet me by the wishing well!

Bounce-bounce-bouncing with excitement,
Clover

illustration.description: A wise brown rabbit with long ears pressed against a small brass door at the base of the clock tower. A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes floats nearby reading a letter. Musical note symbols show the buzzing sound. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Clover the rabbit listening at the clock door while Boo reads her letter

pages.text:
Dear Clever Clover,

The wishing well! Of course! People drop coins that go jingle-jangle-jingle!

I floated down, down, down into the dark well. My ghostly glow lit up the stone walls like a lantern. And there, hanging on a tiny hook behind the old bucket - a brass key!

It jingled like wind chimes when I carried it up! Jingle-jangle-jing!

We unlocked the little door and - SURPRISE! - out buzzed a family of clockwork bees! Buzz-buzz-BUZZ! They'd been trapped inside, trying to fly but bumping into gears!

The mama bee did a happy wiggle-dance. The papa bee buzzed 'Thank you!' The baby bees zoomed in loop-de-loops!

But Clover, the clock still won't tick! The gears are covered in honey - sticky, gooey, golden honey! The bees must have been making their breakfast.

How do we clean honey off clock gears without breaking anything?

Buzzed and bothered,
Boo

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes floating up from a stone well holding a brass key. Magical clockwork bees with tiny gears on their wings buzz around in celebration, trailing sparkles. The rabbit Clover watches from above. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Boo emerging from the well with a key while clockwork bees fly free

pages.text:
Dear Wonderful Boo,

Honey-covered gears! What a sweet problem!

I remembered something from my garden days - warm water makes honey flow like syrup! So I hopped to the village fountain and filled my water bucket.

But you had the BEST idea! You made your ghostly self extra warm and glowy, then gently breathed on the gears - WHOOSH! The honey melted and dripped away like golden raindrops!

The clockwork bees helped too! They buzzed around the gears, their tiny wings creating perfect little breezes to dry everything clean!

Then - TICK! The first gear turned! TOCK! The second gear spun! TICK-TOCK-TICK-TOCK!

The clock hands started moving again! The village bell went DING-DONG-DING!

Everyone cheered! Speedy Sam Snail did victory laps! Mrs. Maple's new bread smelled perfect! Even the clockwork bees found a new home in the village garden!

We did it together, dear friend!

Tick-tock grateful,
Clover

P.S. - Want to help me build a bee hotel next?

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes glowing warmly near spinning clock gears while a brown rabbit pours water. Clockwork bees buzz happily around clean gears. The clock hands move again as villagers cheer below. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Boo and Clover working together to clean the clock gears with celebrating villagers

metadata.lessonPlan: Discuss how machines work, what happens when they break, and how friends can solve problems together through observation and experimentation.

coverPrompt.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes floating near a tall village clock tower. Snails with tiny racing helmets zoom past flowering cottages in a dreamy village setting. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
coverPrompt.alt: Boo the friendly ghost near a clock tower with racing snails in a whimsical village

questions.question: What sound did Clover hear that Boo missed?
questions.explanation: Clover's sensitive bunny ears heard the buzzing of trapped clockwork bees inside the clock!

questions.question: Where did they find the key to open the clock?
questions.explanation: Boo found the brass key hanging on a hook inside the wishing well!

questions.question: What was stuck on the clock gears?
questions.explanation: The clockwork bees had covered the gears with sticky, golden honey!

questions.question: How did Boo help clean the honey off the gears?
questions.explanation: Boo made her ghostly self extra warm and glowy, then breathed on the gears to melt the honey!

questions.question: What did the clockwork bees do to help?
questions.explanation: The clockwork bees used their tiny wings to create perfect little breezes that helped dry the gears clean!
